title: The weighted error to a constant field is nonnegative

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If both cell masses are nonnegative, the weighted squared error from a two-cell field to any constant c is nonnegative.
assumptions: c is any real constant comparison value
informal.expected_conclusion: The weighted constant-approximation error is nonnegative.

declarations.name: weightedConstApproxError2
declarations.statement: def weightedConstApproxError2 (m0 m1 u0 u1 c : Real) : Real := m0 * (u0 - c) ^ 2 + m1 * (u1 - c) ^ 2

declarations.name: weightedConstApproxError2_nonneg
declarations.statement: theorem weightedConstApproxError2_nonneg (m0 m1 u0 u1 c : Real) (hm0 : 0 <= m0) (hm1 : 0 <= m1) : 0 <= weightedConstApproxError2 m0 m1 u0 u1 c

tasks.input: Translate nonnegativity of weighted squared error to a constant field into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem weightedConstApproxError2_nonneg (m0 m1 u0 u1 c : Real) (hm0 : 0 <= m0) (hm1 : 0 <= m1) : 0 <= weightedConstApproxError2 m0 m1 u0 u1 c

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding the weighted constant-error expression.
tasks.target: Unfold weightedConstApproxError2 and use mul_nonneg, sq_nonneg, and nlinarith.
